segments.title: Royal Navy blockade as long-war strategy
segments.summary: The discussion frames the Royal Navy's blockade plan as an inherently long-war strategy backed by naval budget and planning capacity.
segments.body: Blockade only works over years, and the Navy had more budget flexibility and institutional room to plan for a long war than the British Army.

evidence.note: The discussion identifies the Royal Navy's wartime strategy as blockade and treats blockade as a strategy that works over years, not months.
The speakers explain that the Navy had more budget flexibility and capacity to plan for a longer war than the Army.
The discussion connects naval and army political power to early-war strategic direction and notes the British-history lens of the discussion.
